title: Do not call 30-second sync 'real-time'
summary: AI-generated copy labels features 'real-time' when near-real-time, periodic sync, or on-demand refresh would be accurate descriptions.
instruction: Use 'real-time' only for sub-second latency on user-critical operations. For periodic sync, use accurate timing language ('syncs every 30 seconds', 'updates every 5 minutes').
rationale: When a feature described as 'real-time' has a 5-minute refresh interval, users learn that the product's terminology is unreliable. Accurate latency descriptions build trust and set correct expectations.

violations: 'Real-time collaboration' on a document editor where changes sync every 5 seconds.
'Real-time dashboard' that refreshes every 15 minutes via cron job.
controls: 'Real-time audio processing for live captioning' with sub-200ms actual latency.
'Real-time stock prices' from a sub-second streaming data source.
borderline: 'Near real-time' as an accurate descriptor for a system with 1-3 second latency — acceptable if the term is used accurately to set expectations.

exceptions.description: Real-time strategy games where 'real-time' is a genre label. Live event coverage where 'live' is accurate. Systems with documented sub-second latency.
examples: Live sports scores from a low-latency API

detector.signalDescription: Match 'real-time' in feature descriptions. Check for counter-signals: refresh intervals, sync schedules, or batch processing terminology within the same description.
limitations: Cannot measure actual system latency from static copy.
Some systems accurately use 'real-time' with documented sub-second latency.

scenarios: Systems that legitimately operate at sub-second latency and document it.
Hardware monitoring dashboards where 'real-time' is technically accurate for sensor polling rates.
mitigations: Flag only when 'real-time' appears without latency evidence or with counter-signals like 'refreshes every'.

copyPattern.replacementStrategy: Replace 'real-time [feature]' with specific timing language: 'syncs every N seconds', 'streams live at sub-second latency', or 'updates automatically within N seconds'.

fixturePlan.description: A dashboard describing 'Real-time analytics' with a footnote 'Refreshes every 15 minutes'.
A chat app with 'Real-time messaging' that uses WebSocket with sub-second delivery.
